titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Peter Burton Papers
titlestmt.author: by Nora Soto

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday November 28, 2024 at 00:06

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection contains writings, publishing files, correspondence, photographs, personal papers, printed material, and computer and audiovisual media documenting the life and literary career of British writer and editor Peter Burton.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection contains writings, publishing files, correspondence, photographs, personal papers, printed material, and computer and audiovisual media documenting the life and literary career of British writer and editor Peter Burton. Writings include notes, drafts, and proofs for Burton's books, articles, interviews, and reviews, as well as anthologies he edited. Publishing files consist of professional correspondence, contracts, publicity files, and other papers pertaining to Burton's work as editor at Gay Men's Press and Millivres Books. Included are drafts of books by Roger Baker, Francis King, Robin Maugham, and Ian Young. Correspondence includes letters from other gay writers such as Hugh Fleetwood and John Mitzel. Photographs portray Burton's friends and family, Beverley Nichols, Gerald Hamilton, and Denis Lemon, and scenes of popular nightclubs in Brighton, England. Also present in the collection are long-playing records by Rod Stewart and Faces.

bioghist.head: Peter Burton (1945–2011)
bioghist.p: Peter Burton was a British writer and editor known as a pioneering figure in gay journalism in the United Kingdom. He contributed to Spartacus and Jeremy , two of the earliest openly gay magazines founded after the decriminalization of male homosexuality in Britain in 1967. In 1973, he began writing for Gay News , and he worked as a literary editor and personal assistant to editor Denis Lemon from 1973 until the newspaper ceased publication in 1983. He also co-founded the short-lived Gay Reporter with Lemon in 1983, which was then merged into Him Magazine . From 1983 to 2003, Burton was literary and features editor of the magazine Gay Times .

Born on April 29, 1945 in Hackney, London, Burton left school at the age of 15 and began working in the publicity department of publishing house Hamish Hamilton. From 1966 to 1968, he managed the London gay bar Le Duce. After meeting novelist Robin Maugham in 1968, Burton worked as his personal assistant until Maugham's death in 1981. Throughout the 1970s, Burton also worked intermittently as personal assistant and publicist to Billy Gaff, the manager of Rod Stewart and Faces.

Burton was an editor at Gay Men's Press, the first LGBT publisher in Britain, and its successor Millivres Books, where he edited six anthologies of gay short stories, three of which were nominated for the Lambda Literary Award: The Mammoth Book of Gay Short Stories (1997); Bend Sinister: The Gay Times Book of Disturbing Stories (2002); and Serendipity: The Gay Times Book of New Stories (2004). He also edited Talking to… (1991), a collection of his interviews with other LGBT writers such as Quentin Crisp, Patricia Highsmith, Christopher Isherwood, and Larry Kramer. He wrote two memoirs, Parallel Lives (1985) and Amongst the Aliens: Some Aspects of a Gay Life (1995), as well as the authorized biography Rod Stewart: A Life on the Town (1977). As a journalist, Burton contributed to a variety of magazines, including English Penthouse , Gay Sunshine , The London Magazine , and Q International . He wrote book reviews for Daily Express from 2005 to 2011.
Burton died on November 7, 2011 in Brighton, England, where he lived for most of his life.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into seven series: I. Writings, 1940s–2011. II. Publishing Files, 1968–2011. III. Correspondence, 1974–2011. IV. Photographs, 1938–2000s. V. Personal Papers, 1891–2011. VI. Printed Material, 1970–2009. VII. Computer and Audiovisual Media, 1960s–1990s.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 19 (electronic media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 20-24 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Peter Burton Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Torstan Højer on the James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection Fund and the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2017.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Peter Burton Papers. James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Printed material received with the collection was removed for separate cataloging and can be accessed by searching the library's online catalog.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of drafts, notes, correspondence, research files, publicity files, and other materials related to books, anthologies, plays, articles, interviews, obituaries, reviews, and other works written or edited by Burton. Books include the memoir Parallel Lives , the biography Rod Stewart: A Life on the Town , and the Gay Men's Press anthology Bend Sinister: The Gay Times Book of Disturbing Stories . The series also includes Burton's transcriptions of Robin Maugham's diaries.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Baker, Roger. Drag: A History of Female Impersonation in the Performing Arts
Clare, Frank (Frederick W. Clayton). The Cloven Pine
County, Jane. Man Enough to Be a Woman
Elwood, Nick. All the Queen's Men
Garland, Rodney. The Heart in Exile
Gordon, John R. Warriors and Outlaws

Harper, Simon. My Life as a Punchline
Jones, John Sam. With Angels and Furies
Mau, Michael P. Sons of Confucius

Wright, Thomas E. Dancing with Guerillas
Yeo, Robin, editor. All Boys Together
Zetter, Andy. Tales of a Hollywood Wannabe

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains Burton's correspondence with other writers, artists, publishers, and friends. Notable correspondents include Hugh Fleetwood, Francis King, John Mitzel, and Ian Young.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of Burton's personal and family photographs, negatives, and slides, as well as photographs collected by Burton as references for various writing projects. Included are publicity stills for Burton's film and theater reviews, promotional portraits, and headshots. Other subjects are nightclubs in Brighton, England, and gay landmarks throughout England. The series also includes Robin Maugham's portraits of his friend Gerald Hamilton in Spain and photographs of the writer Beverley Nichols.

did.unittitle: Beverley Nichols and Cyril Butcher photograph album
Beverley Nichols and Cyril Butcher photograph album
Gerald Hamilton photographs by Robin Maugham

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains address books, appointment books, and other personal documents belonging to Burton. Also included are papers of Robin Maugham and a painting by Hugh Fleetwood.

did.unittitle: Untitled painting on canvas by Hugh Fleetwood

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains clippings, funeral programs, magazines, postcards, press kits, theater programs, and other printed matter collected by Burton for reference.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains compact discs, floppy disks, audiocassettes, and long-playing records. There are recordings of Beverley Nichols as well as commercial musical albums of Rod Stewart and his band Faces.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Computer Media, and Audiovisual Media. Audiovisual Media is arranged alphabetically by format.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 19 (electronic media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 20-24 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
